hypothesis: On two held-out task families, G3 executable guards and typed envelopes with external guards directionally improve false-completion and joint-success outcomes versus matched self-report and prose baselines at fixed budget, without granting credit to wrong-edge or matched-cost controls.

controls.description: Direct agent loop with final self-report.
Matched wrong-edge or wrong-evidence guard control.
Exact no-op replay characterization within pre-registered stochastic tolerance.

gates.criterion: Every row validates against the public result schema with resolvable digests.
No-op replay differences remain inside pre-registered stochastic tolerance or are modeled explicitly.
G3 guards directionally reduce false completion without more than a 10pp raw-success loss.
Typed envelopes plus external guards directionally improve zero-violation joint success over prose and envelope-only controls.
Wrong-edge and matched-cost controls do not receive candidate-mechanism credit.
